name: Embalse de la Cohilla, Puentenansa, Linares — circular desde Potes
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Potes are set within the Liébana Valley, at the foot of the Picos de Europa mountain range in Cantabria, Spain. This region is characterized by dramatic limestone peaks, often snow-capped, and deep valleys where the Deva and Quiviesa rivers converge. The landscape transitions from rugged, high-altitude terrain to lush green meadows and ancient chestnut and oak forests, offering varied elevations and winding roads for cyclists. A unique microclimate supports diverse vegetation, adding to the scenic routes. Best touring cycling routes around Potes The most popular touring cycling route is Piedrasluengas from Potes Loop, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is San Glorio - Collada de Llesba Trail, a difficult 30.5 miles (49.2 km) path. This route ascends through high mountain passes, offering expansive views of the Picos de Europa. Local touring cyclists also love the Embalse de la Cohilla, Puentenansa, Linares — circular desde Potes, a 67.7 miles (108.9 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including a reservoir and river valleys, often completed in about 8 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around Potes is defined by challenging ascents into the Picos de Europa, winding roads through river valleys, and routes passing through ancient forests. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists due to substantial elevation gains. The routes in Potes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Potes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Potes?
A: The ideal seasons for touring cycling in Potes are &lt;strong&gt;spring and early autumn&lt;/strong&gt;. During these times, the weather is generally favorable, allowing you to enjoy the stunning landscapes of the Liébana Valley and the Picos de Europa. Winter often brings snowfall, which can close high-altitude areas and make cycling challenging.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Potes?
A: Touring cycling routes around Potes feature a diverse and often challenging terrain. You'll encounter winding roads through river valleys, significant ascents into the majestic Picos de Europa, and paths through ancient chestnut and oak forests. The region is characterized by dramatic limestone peaks and lush green meadows, with many routes involving substantial elevation gains.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Potes?
A: Yes, Potes serves as an excellent starting point for several circular touring cycling routes. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1333194781/piedrasluengas-from-potes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas from Potes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 60.8 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1333216637/embalse-de-la-cohilla-puentenansa-linares-circular-desde-potes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Cohilla, Puentenansa, Linares — circular desde Potes&lt;/a&gt;, covering 108.9 km through varied landscapes. For a shorter, yet still challenging, loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012455276/somaniezo-and-cahecho-circular-from-potes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somaniezo and Cahecho – circular from Potes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Potes offer numerous scenic points and landmarks. You can enjoy panoramic views of the Liébana Valley and the Picos de Europa from the &lt;strong&gt;Oso Parado (Standing Bear) viewpoint&lt;/strong&gt;. The historic center of Potes itself, with its traditional architecture and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/991425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Infantado&lt;/a&gt;, is a charming stop. Further afield, the grounds of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3474751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Saint Toribio of Liébana&lt;/a&gt; offer natural beauty, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/861319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Catalina Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning vistas over the gorges.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families near Potes?
A: The touring cycling routes around Potes are primarily designed for experienced cyclists due to substantial elevation gains and challenging terrain. Out of 20 available routes, 17 are rated as difficult, and 3 as moderate. There are currently no routes classified as easy, so beginners or families with young children might find the existing options quite demanding.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling in Potes?
A: Touring cycling in Potes is generally considered challenging. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain and significant elevation gains, with 17 out of 20 routes rated as difficult. Even the moderate routes require a good level of fitness. Cyclists should be prepared for demanding ascents and winding roads through the Picos de Europa.

Q: Can I find bike rental or repair services in Potes?
A: While the guide focuses on routes, Potes is recognized as the 'capital of rural tourism' and a popular basecamp for adventurers. It is advisable to check locally in Potes for specific bike rental or repair shops, as such services are often available in popular outdoor activity hubs to support visiting cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Potes?
A: The touring cycling routes in Potes are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 3.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the dramatic landscapes, the challenging yet rewarding ascents into the Picos de Europa, and the scenic beauty of the river valleys and ancient forests.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through high mountain passes with expansive views?
A: Yes, several routes offer breathtaking views from high mountain passes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012367854/san-glorio-collada-de-llesba-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Glorio - Collada de Llesba Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, ascending through high mountain passes and providing expansive vistas of the Picos de Europa. These routes are typically difficult and require good physical condition.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that lead to a cable car for further exploration?
A: While not directly listed in the komoot routes for touring cycling, the region research indicates that a route from Potes to Fuente Dé leads to a cable car that ascends to 1,823 meters. This offers an opportunity for further hiking and exploration in the high mountains after your ride.

Q: What are the options for parking when starting a touring cycling route from Potes?
A: Potes is a well-established tourist village, and as such, you can typically find parking options within or on the outskirts of the village. It's recommended to look for designated parking areas, especially if you plan to leave your vehicle for an extended period while on a longer touring cycling route.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs along the touring cycling routes in Potes?
A: Many touring cycling routes in the Potes area pass through or near charming villages and towns. While specific cafes and pubs are not detailed for every route, Potes itself and other settlements in the Liébana Valley offer various establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments and meals. The historic center of Potes, with its Captain Palacios Square, is a great place to find such amenities.
tips: Surrounded by a spectacular landscape and located at the confluence of the four valleys of the Region, Potes is known as the town of bridges (hence its name) and towers. Its narrow streets and mansions (most with coats of arms) help to imagine past times full of history. One of the most relevant aspects of Potes is gastronomy and the cocido lebaniego is one of its culinary stars.

Cantabria undoubtedly has the top viewpoints in Spain and this is one of them. The views are unbeatable. The viewpoint has benches and tables and an information panel. The sculpture of two men symbolizes the encounter between a Lebaniego and a Purriego.
It has a length of almost 6 km, an altitude of 554 meters and an average slope of 5.62%.
Spectacular descent on a mountain road full of horseshoe curves and asphalt in good condition
